variables.USER_FOCUS: High-intensity adversarial review for a Go Ethereum execution client fork used in OP Stack and Mantle L2 contexts, with consensus, EVM, RPC, database, and node-operator safety concerns.
REPOSITORY_PROFILE: Go Ethereum fork for execution-client functionality in OP Stack and Mantle L2 contexts.
Go module: github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum; Go version is tracked in go.mod.
Primary executable surface includes geth and related cmd utilities; core packages include EVM, state, trie, txpool, miner, consensus, p2p, RPC, accounts, eth, core/rawdb, and node services.
Build and checks commonly involve Makefile targets such as make geth or make all, plus Go unit/integration tests.

Public contracts: geth CLI flags, node configuration, genesis/chain config handling, JSON-RPC APIs, Engine API behavior where applicable, database format, P2P protocol behavior, metrics, and account/signer APIs.

Consensus-critical risk areas: EVM execution, gas accounting, block/header validation, chain config and fork activation, receipts/logs, state root correctness, transaction pool behavior, and reorg handling.

OP Stack/Mantle risk areas: L2 chain configuration, deposit and system transaction handling, fee accounting, native-token assumptions, rollup compatibility, Engine API integration, and deviations from upstream go-ethereum behavior.
Storage and recovery risk areas: LevelDB/ancient data handling, snapshots, trie/state corruption, migrations, pruning, crash recovery, and backward compatibility for existing node data directories.
Network/RPC risk areas: malformed P2P messages, DoS surfaces, RPC compatibility, trace/debug APIs, HTTP/WS/IPC exposure, auth boundaries, and error visibility.
Operational risk areas: node startup/shutdown behavior, resource leaks, sync performance, memory/disk growth, metrics, logging, and behavior under degraded peers or RPC clients.
Review should treat consensus, state, database, networking, and RPC changes as high blast-radius even when tests pass.
